title: Send all eligible leads in scheduled cadence
description: Change the scheduled n8n cadence from exactly-one guaranteed delivery to sending all eligible leads when multiple leads are available, while preserving cadence idempotency and Slack/email delivery behavior.
close_reason: Changed backend cadence delivery to send all eligible candidates in one Slack/email batch with per-lead delivery records, deployed rebuilt groupscout_app, updated live n8n workflow to delivery_mode all_eligible, and sent a one-off batch catch-up: 7 Slack leads delivered and recorded; remaining eligible candidates is 0. Email copy to sales_vanair@sandman.ca failed with Resend 403 until a sending domain is verified (tracked by groupscout-site-7ak).

title: Add Sandman sales email to lead notifications
description: Add sales_vanair@sandman.ca to LEAD_NOTIFY_EMAILS so guaranteed cadence lead deliveries email both the existing recipient and the Sandman sales inbox, then verify runtime config after restart.
close_reason: Added sales_vanair@sandman.ca to backend .env LEAD_NOTIFY_EMAILS alongside the existing recipient, recreated groupscout_app, and verified the live container has both recipients and health is OK. Note: EMAIL_FROM remains onboarding@resend.dev, so Resend may require a verified sender domain for non-owner delivery.

title: Run manual June 24 cadence catch-up
description: Run a manual guaranteed one-lead /run request for lead-cadence:2026-06-24:wednesday after fixing the n8n cadence payload, then verify a delivery row was recorded.
close_reason: Ran manual guaranteed /run catch-up for lead-cadence:2026-06-24:wednesday. Backend returned delivery_status=sent and recorded lead_deliveries row for Pattullo Bridge Replacement Project.

title: Fix n8n cadence payload for guaranteed lead delivery
description: The active GroupScout daily lead cadence workflow calls POST /run with an empty JSON body, so the backend runs normal collect/enrich mode instead of guaranteed one-lead delivery. Update the workflow payload to include the cadence key/idempotency key and guaranteed delivery fields, then re-import/reactivate the n8n workflow.
close_reason: Fixed the tracked and live n8n cadence workflow so POST /run sends guaranteed-delivery cadence fields; re-imported, reactivated, and restarted n8n; verified live export is active with the fixed payload.

title: Fix duplicate lead insertion from scheduled /run
description: The June 14, 2026 n8n cadence executed once successfully, but the backend inserted 73 leads and notified 23, including repeated Pattullo Bridge, RW Bruhn Bridge, Quartz Creek, Roads Bridges and Goods Movement, Eventbrite, Delta, and Richmond permit entries. Live Postgres shows raw_projects is empty while raw_projects.raw_data is jsonb, and app logs show every legacy raw project insert failing with JSON/UTF-8 errors. Because normal /run dedup checks raw_projects.hash before enrichment, dedup is effectively bypassed across runs and within a run when collectors emit duplicate cards. Fix raw_projects persistence/schema drift, add same-run and lead-level canonical duplicate protection, and consider run idempotency for scheduled /run so n8n retries cannot resend an already completed digest.
close_reason: Root cause was raw_projects.raw_data being jsonb while collectors write non-JSON/non-UTF-8 bytes (PDF/HTML), so every raw insert failed silently, leaving raw_projects empty and bypassing hash dedup. Fixed in backend commit 1385271 (branch task/fix-scheduled-run-dedup, fast-forwarded into main): migrated raw_projects.raw_data to bytea (migration 011) and added LeadStore.ExistsBySourceTitle as a lead-level dedup check before any insert, covering same-run and cross-run duplicates. Also found and fixed a separate deploy blocker: Dockerfile only compiled cmd/server/main.go, not the whole package, so no image build had succeeded since cmd/server was split into multiple files weeks ago -- meaning the already-closed ei7 fix never actually reached production either. Fixed Dockerfile, rebuilt and redeployed groupscout_app, verified migration applied and app healthy. Backed up production Postgres before migrating, then deduped the 272 existing live leads rows down to 70 (kept earliest row per source+title plus any rows with real lead_deliveries/outreach_log references) so historical duplicates don't linger. All unit and Postgres-backed integration tests pass.

title: Implement neumorphic UI reskin
description: Replace the prior cyberpunk/glitch frontend skin with the supplied Neumorphism Soft UI system across the dependency-free GroupScout UI renderer, generated static assets, design token contract, and runtime tests. Preserve same-origin API boundaries, accessibility, responsive behavior, no browser secrets, and the existing source architecture.
close_reason: Replaced the prior cyberpunk skin with the supplied Neumorphism Soft UI prompt in frontend commit 8fdfde1. Quality gates: npm run build; node --test test/design-tokens.test.js test/phase-13-renderer-runtime.test.js; npm test; git diff --check.

Verified groupscout_app is running, host /health returns HTTP 200, n8n can reach http://groupscout:8080/health, and the active n8n cadence workflow still matches the Sunday/Wednesday guaranteed-delivery contract.

title: Resolve Beads directory permission warning on Windows mount
description: bd reports /mnt/c/Users/alvin/groupscout-site/.beads as mode 0777 and recommends 0700. chmod 700 did not change the reported mode on this Windows-mounted workspace, so decide whether to enable WSL metadata, move the Beads workspace to a POSIX filesystem, or document the accepted limitation.
close_reason: Accepted and documented the Windows-mounted coordination checkout limitation: chmod 700 .beads exits successfully but stat still reports 777 on the current DrvFs/9p mount without metadata support. Future agents should ignore the warning unless the checkout moves to POSIX storage or WSL metadata support is enabled.

title: Investigate normal-run collector and raw persistence warnings

value: Backend Dockerfile built only cmd/server/main.go (not the whole package) since cmd/server was split into multiple files weeks ago, so no docker image build had succeeded since then -- every fix merged after that split (including the closed ei7 issue) never reached the live container. Fixed by building ./cmd/server instead. Always verify a docker build actually succeeds before trusting that a merged/closed fix is live.

main branch in groupscout backend was 11+ commits behind several feature branches (task/event-driven-ingest, task/audit-retention-worker, task/guaranteed-lead-delivery, task/fix-scheduled-run-dedup) -- closed issues ei7 and crz referenced fixes that only existed on those unmerged branches. When closing a backend issue, check whether the fix commit is actually an ancestor of main and whether the live container's image predates it, not just that the commit exists somewhere.

To change a live n8n workflow's schedule in the groupscout_n8n container: export with 'n8n export:workflow --id=<id>' (output has a leading 'Error tracking disabled' log line to strip before JSON parse), edit triggerAtDay (0=Sun..6=Sat) and rename node+connection key+workflow name, then 'n8n import:workflow --input=file'. NOTE: import DEACTIVATES the workflow, so follow with 'n8n update:workflow --id=<id> --active=true' and 'docker restart groupscout_n8n' for the schedule trigger to re-register. The cadence workflow id groupscout-sunday-wednesday-lead-cadence and asset filename sunday-wednesday-lead-cadence.json are retained for stability even though it now runs daily except Saturday.

User wants NO 'Co-Authored-By' trailers in git commit messages, in both groupscout (backend) and groupscout-site (coordination) repos. This OVERRIDES the harness default and the CLAUDE.md 'End git commit messages with Co-Authored-By: Claude...' instruction. Do not add Co-Authored-By or any AI attribution trailer to commits. On 2026-06-17 all existing co-authored commits in both repos were stripped via filter-branch and force-pushed.
